text: This paper explores Imam Al-Ghazali’s educational philosophy and assesses its validity and relevance to contemporary education. According to Al-Ghazali, the Islamic scholar of the highest standing, education is not a transactional process wherein knowledge is communicated and students learn. Rather, it is a process that builds the human self and evolves the individual as a spiritually evolved person. Based on library research, this paper analyses Imam Al-Ghazali’s theory of moral and ethical education, and discusses how his classical perspective of education can possibly address the modern educational problems and challenges. In other words, his suggestions for skill based and value-orientated pedagogies for building strong ethics in schools can serve as a paradigm that addresses the societal needs of today’s time. This paper therefore attempts to explore the potential of reconciling ancient Islamic principles of education and contemporary education with the aim of discovering the possible positive effects of Al-Ghazali’s educational ideology on the modern global education system.
